seo.title: Building Code Generation with Mastra + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Mastra + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Mastra provides a powerful TypeScript framework for building AI agents with workflows, knowledge bases, and integrations, making it ideal for code generation use cases where agents iteratively produce, test, and refine code. Guardrails are essential in this high-risk scenario to prevent syntax errors, unsafe code execution, credential leaks, and malformed outputs that could lead to production failures or security breaches. Vex integrates seamlessly as middleware to verify agent outputs, ensuring reliable code generation pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Mastra
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Mastra code generation architecture involves an LLM agent workflow that receives a user prompt, generates initial code using tools like code interpreters, executes it in a sandbox, validates tests, and iterates based on feedback. Mastra's workflow steps handle transitions between generation, execution, and refinement phases, with memory retrieval providing conversation history and knowledge bases supplying language-specific patterns.

Agent tools integrate with sandbox environments (e.g., Docker or Node.js sandboxes) for safe execution, while verification steps check syntax, safety, and functionality. Memory management ensures context relevance, but without validation, stale data or malformed tool outputs can derail iterations.

Vex middleware intercepts workflow outputs at key transitions, applying checks for code safety, syntax, and dependencies before proceeding.

risks.name: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
risks.description: Mastra workflows pass unverified code between generation, execution, and refinement steps, allowing syntax errors to propagate.
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware validates outputs at each transition using syntax parsers and safety scanners.

risks.name: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
risks.description: Sandbox execution tools return invalid JSON or partial code snippets, confusing downstream LLM refinement.
risks.mitigation: Vex parses and normalizes tool outputs before injecting into workflow context.

risks.name: Memory retrieval injecting stale context
risks.description: Previous iteration code snippets pollute context, causing agents to regenerate flawed solutions.
risks.mitigation: Vex filters memory retrievals to include only verified, relevant code artifacts.

risks.name: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
risks.description: Third-party API responses (e.g., npm package data) contain unexpected formats, derailing code generation.
risks.mitigation: Vex schemas enforce expected data shapes from integrations before agent processing.
